text:
*[Bug 18989] Wishlist: provide conversion of POSIXlt time zone retaining sub-second accuracy*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18989>

--- Comment #33 from Suharto Anggono (<mailto:suharto_anggono@yahoo.com|suharto_anggono@yahoo.com>) ---
In tests/datetime3.R ,
        show_nonTRUE(
            xG.$sec[c(7, 25)] == x25$sec[c(7, 25)] )
can be replaced with
        xG.$sec[7] == x25$sec[7]
.
Tests about 'wday' component can be removed.


From Comment 20, maybe for additional tests:


lcx. <- lcx
lcx[1:25] <- x25[1:25]
lcx.[[7]] <- x25[[7]]
lcx.[[25]] <- x25[[25]]

Expected as is:
lcx[1:25] - x25[1:25] == 0  # ok in R 4.6.0
lcx$sec == x25$sec  # not in R 4.6.x, except thtz="UTC"
lcx$wday == x25$wday  # not in R 4.6.x, except thtz="UTC"
lcx.[[7]] - x25[[7]] == 0  # ok in R 4.6.0
lcx.[[25]] - x25[[25]] == 0  # ok in R 4.6.0
lcx.$sec[c(7, 25)] == x25$sec[c(7, 25)]  # not in R 4.6.x, except thtz="UTC"
lcx.$wday[c(7, 25)] == x25$wday  # not in R 4.6.x, except thtz="UTC"


xU. <- xU <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(numeric(25), tz = "Etc/UTC"))
xU[1:25] <- x25[1:25]
xU.[[7]] <- x25[[7]]

It is like 'xG' and 'xG.', except that "tzone" attribute has length 3.
Just expect success ([[<- failed in r89926). Results are checked in 'xG' and
xG.'.
Expected (fulfilled in R 4.6.0):
xU[1:25] - x25[1:25] == 0
xU.[[7]] - x25[[7]] == 0


non-large times:


x1 <- as.POSIXlt(Sys.time()); attr(x1, "tzone") <- NULL; x2 <- x1
x1[1L] <- y <- Sys.time()
x2[[1L]] <- y

Failed in R 4.6.0.
Expected:
x1[1L] - y == 0
x2[[1L]] - y == 0


x1 <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(numeric(2))); attr(x1, "tzone") <- NULL
x2 <- x1
y <- list(as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(0, tz = "Etc/UTC")),
          as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(0, tz = "Pacific/Auckland")))
x1[1L] <- y[[1L]]
x1[2L] <- y[[2L]]
x2[[1L]] <- y[[1L]]
x2[[2L]] <- y[[2L]]

Expected (fulfilled in R 4.6.1):
c(x1[1L] - y[[1L]], x1[2L] - y[[2L]]) == 0
!all(identical(c(unclass(x1[1L])), c(unclass(y[[1L]]))),
     identical(c(unclass(x1[2L])), c(unclass(y[[2L]]))))
# c(unclass(*)) removes attributes other than names
c(x2[[1L]] - y[[1L]], x2[[2L]] - y[[2L]]) == 0
!all(identical(c(unclass(x2[[1L]])), c(unclass(y[[1L]]))),
     identical(c(unclass(x2[[2L]])), c(unclass(y[[2L]]))))


character:


x1 <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(numeric(2))); attr(x1, "tzone") <- NULL
x2 <- x1
x1[1:2] <- y <- c("2006-02-20 11:16:16.683000", "9999-12-31 23:59:59.999999")
x2[[1L]] <- y[[1L]]; x2[[2L]] <- y[[2L]]

Expected:
x1[1:2] == y
identical(format(x1, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%OS6"), y)  # not in R 4.6.x
x2[[1L]] == y[[1L]]
x2[[2L]] == y[[2L]]
identical(format(x2, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%OS6"), y)  # not in R 4.6.x


x2 <- x1 <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(numeric(2), tz = "UTC"))
x1[1:2] <- y <- c("2006-02-20 11:16:16.683000", "9999-12-31 23:59:59.999999")
x2[[1L]] <- y[[1L]]; x2[[2L]] <- y[[2L]]

Expected:
x1[1:2] == y  # ok in R 4.6.0
substring(format(x1, "%OS6"), 4) == substring(y, 21)  # not in R 4.6.x
x2[[1L]] == y[[1L]]  # ok in R 4.6.0
x2[[2L]] == y[[2L]]  # ok in R 4.6.0
substring(format(x2, "%OS6"), 4) == substring(y, 21)  # not in R 4.6.x

elements.text: [Bug 18989] Wishlist: provide conversion of POSIXlt time zone retaining sub-second accuracy

) ---
In tests/datetime3.R ,
        show_nonTRUE(
            xG.$sec[c(7, 25)] == x25$sec[c(7, 25)] )
can be replaced with
        xG.$sec[7] == x25$sec[7]
.
Tests about 'wday' component can be removed.



From Comment 20, maybe for additional tests:



lcx. <- lcx
lcx[1:25] <- x25[1:25]
lcx.[[7]] <- x25[[7]]
lcx.[[25]] <- x25[[25]]

Expected as is:
lcx[1:25] - x25[1:25] == 0  # ok in R 4.6.0
lcx$sec == x25$sec  # not in R 4.6.x, except thtz="UTC"
lcx$wday == x25$wday  # not in R 4.6.x, except thtz="UTC"
lcx.[[7]] - x25[[7]] == 0  # ok in R 4.6.0
lcx.[[25]] - x25[[25]] == 0  # ok in R 4.6.0
lcx.$sec[c(7, 25)] == x25$sec[c(7, 25)]  # not in R 4.6.x, except thtz="UTC"
lcx.$wday[c(7, 25)] == x25$wday  # not in R 4.6.x, except thtz="UTC"



xU. <- xU <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(numeric(25), tz = "Etc/UTC"))
xU[1:25] <- x25[1:25]
xU.[[7]] <- x25[[7]]

It is like 'xG' and 'xG.', except that "tzone" attribute has length 3.
Just expect success ([[<- failed in r89926). Results are checked in 'xG' and
xG.'.
Expected (fulfilled in R 4.6.0):
xU[1:25] - x25[1:25] == 0
xU.[[7]] - x25[[7]] == 0



non-large times:



x1 <- as.POSIXlt(Sys.time()); attr(x1, "tzone") <- NULL; x2 <- x1
x1[1L] <- y <- Sys.time()
x2[[1L]] <- y

Failed in R 4.6.0.
Expected:
x1[1L] - y == 0
x2[[1L]] - y == 0



x1 <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(numeric(2))); attr(x1, "tzone") <- NULL
x2 <- x1
y <- list(as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(0, tz = "Etc/UTC")),
          as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(0, tz = "Pacific/Auckland")))
x1[1L] <- y[[1L]]
x1[2L] <- y[[2L]]
x2[[1L]] <- y[[1L]]
x2[[2L]] <- y[[2L]]

Expected (fulfilled in R 4.6.1):
c(x1[1L] - y[[1L]], x1[2L] - y[[2L]]) == 0
!all(identical(c(unclass(x1[1L])), c(unclass(y[[1L]]))),
     identical(c(unclass(x1[2L])), c(unclass(y[[2L]]))))
# c(unclass(*)) removes attributes other than names
c(x2[[1L]] - y[[1L]], x2[[2L]] - y[[2L]]) == 0
!all(identical(c(unclass(x2[[1L]])), c(unclass(y[[1L]]))),
     identical(c(unclass(x2[[2L]])), c(unclass(y[[2L]]))))



character:



x1 <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(numeric(2))); attr(x1, "tzone") <- NULL
x2 <- x1
x1[1:2] <- y <- c("2006-02-20 11:16:16.683000", "9999-12-31 23:59:59.999999")
x2[[1L]] <- y[[1L]]; x2[[2L]] <- y[[2L]]

Expected:
x1[1:2] == y
identical(format(x1, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%OS6"), y)  # not in R 4.6.x
x2[[1L]] == y[[1L]]
x2[[2L]] == y[[2L]]
identical(format(x2, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%OS6"), y)  # not in R 4.6.x



x2 <- x1 <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(numeric(2), tz = "UTC"))
x1[1:2] <- y <- c("2006-02-20 11:16:16.683000", "9999-12-31 23:59:59.999999")
x2[[1L]] <- y[[1L]]; x2[[2L]] <- y[[2L]]

Expected:
x1[1:2] == y  # ok in R 4.6.0
substring(format(x1, "%OS6"), 4) == substring(y, 21)  # not in R 4.6.x
x2[[1L]] == y[[1L]]  # ok in R 4.6.0
x2[[2L]] == y[[2L]]  # ok in R 4.6.0
substring(format(x2, "%OS6"), 4) == substring(y, 21)  # not in R 4.6.x

text:
*[Bug 18989] Wishlist: provide conversion of POSIXlt time zone retaining sub-second accuracy*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18989>

--- Comment #34 from Suharto Anggono (<mailto:suharto_anggono@yahoo.com|suharto_anggono@yahoo.com>) ---
(In reply to Suharto Anggono from comment #33)

!all(identical(c(unclass(.)), c(unclass(.))),
     identical(c(unclass(.)), c(unclass(.))))
is not necessary. It is implied by
c(. - ., . - .) == 0

elements.text: [Bug 18989] Wishlist: provide conversion of POSIXlt time zone retaining sub-second accuracy

) ---
(In reply to Suharto Anggono from comment #33)

!all(identical(c(unclass(.)), c(unclass(.))),
     identical(c(unclass(.)), c(unclass(.))))
is not necessary. It is implied by
c(. - ., . - .) == 0

text:
*[Bug 18989] Wishlist: provide conversion of POSIXlt time zone retaining sub-second accuracy*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18989>

--- Comment #35 from Suharto Anggono (<mailto:suharto_anggono@yahoo.com|suharto_anggono@yahoo.com>) ---
(In reply to Suharto Anggono from comment #31)
Ah, I thought `unCfillPOSIXlt` result always had 11 components. It is not! So,
with

    y <- x2 <- x1 <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(0, tz = "UTC"))
    y$zone <- y$gmtoff <- NULL

,

    x2[[1L]] <- y

still fails.

    x1[1L] <- y

also fails.

elements.text: [Bug 18989] Wishlist: provide conversion of POSIXlt time zone retaining sub-second accuracy

result always had 11 components. It is not! So,
with

    y <- x2 <- x1 <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(0, tz = "UTC"))
    y$zone <- y$gmtoff <- NULL

,

    x2[[1L]] <- y

still fails.

    x1[1L] <- y

also fails.

text:
*[Bug 17549] When building a package, use inRbuildignore() before copying package contents to Tdir*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=17549>

--- Comment #19 from Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) ---
Yes. The pre-copying filtering is very convenient. Maybe with small
improvements to `cleanup_pkg()` the post-`prepare_pkg()` filtering may even
become unnecessary, but we don't know what other packages out there have
`temp_install_pkg()` artefacts in their `.Rbuildignore`. It's safer to keep
filtering twice.

elements.text: [Bug 17549] When building a package, use inRbuildignore() before copying package contents to Tdir

filtering may even
become unnecessary, but we don't know what other packages out there have

. It's safer to keep
filtering twice.

text:

<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=17549>

--- Comment #20 from Kurt Hornik (<mailto:Kurt.Hornik@wu.ac.at|Kurt.Hornik@wu.ac.at>) ---
Thanks.  So I guess we need a new patch which does the full filtering twice?
